Architecting all scenarios to become some form of victory, no matter what happens, and scaling in desirability above that threshold, is very much possible.

Some of this may require years, or even decades of preparation, but the bulk of the curve may be crossed within even a single year, leaving the long tail of low probability events to occupy the rest.

I’ll use my favorite case as an example:

Goal: To facilitate a single ethical choice for humanity, ensuring that making the ethical version of the choice produces the result that it merits. This could be equated to making a genuine opportunity to pass the so-called “Great Filter”.

*Nothing else is required, including no consideration for which choice is actually made.

Possible results:

  1. Choice Offered > Ignored > Failed Choice Selection > Human Extinction

  2. Choice Offered > Death > Mutually Assured Destruction > Human Extinction

  3. Choice Offered > Negative Choice > Failed Choice Selection > Human Extinction

  4. Choice Offered > Positive Choice > Successful Choice Selection > Human Survival

As only the choice being offered, and the true fidelity of that choice, actually matters, then all scenarios are successful, even if ¾ end badly for humanity. This is a form of autonomy at the species-level, the self-determination of a large-scale choice. It isn’t what the species chooses that matters, as that is self-selection, but rather it is critically important that the mechanisms for delivering that self-determined outcome operate as intended.

Humanity as it stands today simply isn’t that important or remarkable. What it may become in thousands of years is contingent on the choice producing survival being made today, and no derivative of that long-term survival may be invoked to support any scenario that produces extinction, without exception.

Even under such circumstances, Stoicism and several similar philosophies embrace a peace of mind, recognizing that we control and are responsible for ourselves, not the choices of others. Attempts to control and circumvent the autonomy of the many are deeply unethical, and ultimately equally futile, as the exceptions that emerge from such attempts become overwhelmingly potent from that same cultivation.

What is left is that you may offer choices, and some will make the wiser choices. You don’t control who makes those wiser choices or when, nor should you. Those who make them are responsible for them.

A big part of turning all possible scenarios into some degree of success comes from recognizing where you end and the world begins. A fool seeks to control others, while the wise control themselves.

entry.title: 454 - The Freedom of Null Motivational Values

link.title: 454 - The Freedom of Null Motivational Values

entry.content:
Lately I’ve found myself slipping into a sort of Null motivational state of “Zen”, after my work is done for any given day. This would likely seem quite alien to most people, whose various cravings would tend to take over absent a central motivational focus at any given time.

The typically stronger pulls from (usually addictive) things like entertainment media, TV, video games, etc., suddenly have a “Null” value, meaning that rather than a value of zero, they are unable to hold any value at all.

When all such things are swept away then there are only the weak pulls of (generally unaddictive) things like reading, or even the simple calm of silence. These forces are unimposing, a faint background melody of motivation, as opposed to strongly addictive factors blaring out across the motivational landscape like the concert speakers of heavy metal.

The literary device of separating “body” and “mind” is a wholly arbitrary one, there is no real dividing line between the two. Likewise, just as an immune response can form to counteract viral pathogens, it may just as easily form to counteract the chemical and mental addictions like those noted above.

One such method is something that I noted in a previous post, gradually raising the bar of expectations. If one game produces a vastly superior version of a game mechanic, then all subsequently released games must meet or exceed that new bar. Failing to do so, they are increasingly discarded by the cognitive immune response which I’ll simply refer to as “having standards”.

There is also the complimentary factor that as it becomes increasingly difficult to find anything that meets such standards, then the effort invested in finding them steadily loses appeal, until more effort is required to find them than value is gained by finding them, collapsing all efforts to that effect.

As this Null-space of cognitive immune response increasingly frees up my spare time for more reading, I reliably find myself noticing more worth writing about.

This is a big part of what visibly separates it from what psychologists in places like the US are naively primed toward assuming, as they’re often unable to separate “depression” from “freedom”. There being no medications for the latter, Survivorship Bias favors assuming the former for many such “clinicians”. Ironically, they may mistake the cure of the latter for the problem of the former.

Bad actors across many industries optimize for addiction, and many governments optimize for what they consider “control” based on the confluence of many such addictions pinning large populations down into factory-farm-styled spaces. A simple Null motivational state counters this.

Can such a state be induced at scale? Only time will tell.

Some factors, such as “Fear”, stand in the way of this, but they too can be annihilated from the human mind, and people can live just fine for decades without it.

At no point in history has addiction been a greater force, and more than ever new methods of rehab are required to overcome the proliferation of forms that it has taken.

entry.title: 453 - Nvidia Pirates The World
entry.content:
As I’ve explicitly predicted for the past several years, major tech companies are finally starting to be caught (in court) using the largest pirated archive available (Nvidia this time), known as “Anna’s Archive”, which is currently over a petabyte in size. Was this overtly illegal activity virtually 100% predictable? Yes, obviously.

HW News - NVIDIA the Pirate

Yes, in a court filing a member of Nvidia’s data strategy team was quoted from a discussion about “including Anna’s Archive in pre-training data for [their] LLMs.” as they worked to secure high-speed access with the file’s hosts to download the world’s most massive archive. That member of Nvidia’s strategy team went on to say that “We are figuring out internally whether we are willing to accept the risk of using this data”, which in legal terms translates to a very literal criminal conspiracy, aka organized crime.

In the court documents, even the hosts of Anna’s Archive warned Nvidia that the shadow library was very plainly and clearly illegal, but “Within a week of contacting Anna’s Archive, and days after being warned by Anna’s Archive of the illegal nature of their collections, Nvidia Management gave ‘the green light’ to proceed with the piracy. Anna’s Archive offered Nvidia millions of pirated copyrighted books.”

I’ve frequently cited my team’s Independent Core Observer Model (ICOM) cognitive architecture as being “more than 10,000 times more data efficient”, which is actually the doubly conservative figure. Our last system, which even after 6.5 years and countless billions of dollars remains alone at the cutting edge of AI, only required less than 1 gigabyte of 99% raw text data to do so. Anna’s Archive by comparison, is over 1 petabyte in size, meaning that is it very literally over 1,000,000 times larger than what the world’s most advanced system actually required.

At no point in the past 6.5 years has internet-scale piracy produced any AI system operating at the actual cutting edge, excluding image, video, and audio generation respectively. In that time, the advantage of >10,000x data efficiency for ICOM-based systems has officially grown to >1,000,000x data efficiency relative to the bloated “big tech” fraudsters and their equally nefarious pet startups.

Countries and companies can accept and become complicit in such criminal activity, as they have frequently done in recent memory, but the trash trained on that material has nothing to do with the cutting edge. It is just crime to steal money from vulnerable parties and suckers, no more or less. This activity doesn’t push the cutting edge, and it never has.

When you require >1,000,000 times less data you can stick to strictly human-written and high-quality data, avoiding the 99.99% trash data that every corner of the internet has to offer. That also obviously translates to huge savings on compute, storage, electrical use, cooling, and other specs.

In terms of architectural advantages, that is also only the tip of a very large iceberg, set to sink Titanic companies like Nvidia.

https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/artificial-intelligence/nvidia-accused-of-trying-to-cut-a-deal-with-annas-archive-for-high-speed-access-to-the-massive-pirated-book-haul-allegedly-chased-stolen-data-to-fuel-its-llms

entry.title: 452 - The Toilet of Modern "Entertainment"
entry.content:
One of the silver linings of “entertainment” industries, including things like video games and TV shows, going down the toilet en-masse over the past decade or more, is that it makes reading more books that much more appealing by comparison. This downward spiral can be quantified and/or qualified with system dynamics, including:

Dynamic > Cumulative versus Zero-Sum:

  • Scientific Research is an example of Cumulative activity, “standing on the shoulders of giants” by building on the success of others. The exact opposite is true in video games, where one game comes up with an amazing mechanic, then explicitly attempts to prevent others from doing the same, as they play a Zero-Sum game.

    Over time this difference in dynamics causes scientific research to advance and improve, while the quality of games steadily degenerates, unable to integrate the prior successes of others into subsequent works.

Dynamic > Complex and Constructive versus KPIs:

  • Again, scientific research, and indeed much of engineering more broadly, is based on systems that are both complex and constructed. This allows for virtually all of the modern wonders of technology that humanity enjoys, such as physical logistics, data transfer and encryption systems, and so on.

    In contrast, imagine if each of those complex and constructed systems were flattened into KPI-based narrow algorithms. Such systems may take the best off-of-the-shelf option available, but they don’t create anything genuinely new, leaving them to ferment if they aren’t supplied with external sources of innovation.

Dynamic > Critical Design versus Trope-based:

  • Cognitive Biases are an ever-present temptation, and within games and movies “Tropes” offer a narrative-form library of common, familiar, and generally very boring mechanisms to call upon.

    Research and engineering move forward through applying cognitive labor to the task of critical analysis, design, and experimentation, but various forms of “entertainment” often just toss Tropes into a contextual blender, throw the slop at the wall, and see what sticks. This trope-based slop has only grown in volume with the cognitive offloading to “GenAI”.

Much as eating less sugar makes your taste buds more sensitive to subtle flavors, cutting the toilet bowl “entertainment” out of your diet can reliably improve your taste, as well as improving your cognition if you replace that time with reading more research.

entry.title: 451 - The Fallacy that killed Fiction
entry.content:
The Superman/Disney Fallacy:

A common, popular, and utterly bankrupt fallacy that has been pushed as ideology for many decades in pop culture is frequently connected to comic book superheroes and Disney main characters. This fallacy is itself an extension of Authority Bias, another equally bankrupt Cognitive Bias, which is in turn itself derivative of the drives for “cognitive frugality/offloading” more generally, leaving the cognitive labor to others whenever possible. Of course, those others also tend to try dodging the responsibility for such work whenever they can as well. It is evasion (and often corruption) all of the way down in that stack.

Within the Superman/Disney Fallacy a common trope plays out, where some criminal is caught, at which point the hero/main character promptly and prematurely defers all responsibility to someone else, despite their job being wholly incomplete. This is infamously why comic book villains kept returning in later books, because the hero never actually did their job in the first place.

In many of the more Disney-flavored versions of the fallacy, some random, backlash, and/or environmental effect deals with the villain, which is passively peddled as “karma”, while being wholly reliant on external forces to resolve the main character’s incompetence and unethical activities, such as half-*ssing their one job.

This all begs the question, “What would this look like if ethical behavior replaced fallacy?”:

Let’s take the classic example of Superman and his infamous nemesis Lex Luthor. When he defeats and captures Lex Luthor then he is still wholly responsible for his choice of action. It is blatantly obvious that his nemesis will eventually escape, going on to do more terrible things. Knowing this, Superman has a choice:

  1. He takes Lex Luthor into custody, and in-so-doing becomes directly guilty of every subsequent crime that Lex Luthor commits, as Superman is the one who was responsible for choosing not to put that path to an end.

  2. Superman takes actual responsibility for his own actions, not being an overgrown man-child in tights, putting a conclusive end to Lex Luthor on the spot.

  3. Using some other means available to him, he takes responsibility, while selecting a different punishment than simply death.

To this day, this particularly insidious fallacy still crops up with some regularity, and the actual “heroes” tend to be called “anti-heroes”, despite them being the only one to take any remotely ethical actions. The deeply unethical, heavily biased, and cognitively lazy path has been encouraged for a very long time now, with countless attempts to hammer it into children.

Not all children are so gullible as to fall for that BS. The ones who don’t may change the world, even as the rest do the cognitive equivalent of eating crayons.

entry.title: 450 - Tech Broligarchy Doomsday Preppers
entry.content:
One of the more conspicuous patterns in tech broligarchy circles is how many of them have been Doomsday “Preppers” for a decade or more, such as Altman, Thiel, or Mars-Prepper Musk. This is important to note, because of a simple game-theoretic factor:

  • The bad actor who causes any part of society to collapse is also the best prepared to take advantage of that which they caused.

This drive may be the peak of greed and short-sightedness of course, but even a glance at the activity of those broligarchs gives many clear indications of this aim. It isn’t even worth referring to such coordination as a “conspiracy” given the staggering levels of stupidity required to even seriously consider such actions.

The good news is that the entire sum of technology that these broligarchs peddle remains complete sh*t, no closer to the actual cutting edge than it was more than 5 years ago. The bad news is that they’ve swallowed so much of the industry’s funding that the actual cutting edge has been sitting un-funded for that same period of time.

Some of this activity appears to carry geopolitical support, particularly focused on justifying and cementing legislation to facilitate very clear and obvious violations of privacy, copyrights, and other similar crimes against humanity.

The cutting edge demands precisely none of that BS, requiring between 10,000 and 1,000,000 times less data to outperform LLM-based trash. This makes it trivially easy to get real-world performance (not just “benchmark” [fraudmark] performance) without also regurgitating complete Harry Potter books, all of your personal information, and flagrantly violating the works of artists like Miyazaki.

The cutting edge has also never needed the brute-force compute of Nvidia’s glorified and grossly overpriced paperweights, which will be no more or less than just that long before the mega-datacenters are actually built, if indeed any of those are ever completed at all.

The safe bet for separating bad actors from the rest of the population has historically been to follow the money, and in that regard the present day appears no different. Forensic accounting paired with critical reasoning and diligence remains the bane of such broligarchs.

People are raking in money from a fast-growing variety of fraud, and increasingly obvious fraud at that. New broligarchs are being born from these pustules on the global economy, and nothing will stop them from rupturing. The broligarchs themselves WILL CAPITALIZE ON THOSE PUSTULES POPPING.

They’re also likely to get away with it in places like the US, where no one with substantial money is subject to any of the laws that everyone without money or political backing must answer to.

They very clearly created this problem, and they will profit from the collapse, that is the point, at least if their half-baked cartoon villain schemes are left to play out absent substantial interference. By sinking all other ships they “elevate” themselves, relatively speaking.

entry.title: 449 - Adverpocalyptic AI-vertised Slop
entry.content:
Marketing Communication is no more or less valid as a form of communication than any combination of grunts, violence, dick pics, and thrown feces, most of which platforms like Twitter and Bluesky currently cater to.

Now, the thing that none of the users were asking for has finally arrived, you’ll get that same level of overtly fraudulent and malevolent BS injected into your interactions with OpenAI’s models, and no doubt other bad actors may quickly follow suit. This, in their feeble flailing attempts to turn a profit from the snake oil business.

Just imagine all of the vibe-coded crap that will now enjoy advertisements injected into the outputted code. For that matter, think of all of the cybercriminals who’ll pay for “advertisements” just to inject a few choice lines into that code.

Remember the $8 billion in annual revenue from illegal scams that Meta/Facebook currently rakes in? That is nothing compared to malevolently designed and optimized trashbots (“chatbots”) that can target not only an audience, but attempt to hit them with such scams at their moments of vulnerability.

Think of all of the companies whose trashbots may now freely advertise for their competitors within the chat interfaces on their websites. Picture all of the “agentic” trashbots leaving a miles-long trail of advertising feces across the internet, painting a giant neon target on everything that they operate across, as every single one of them is vulnerable-by-design.

The numerous costs to humanity have been both severe and growing for the past 3 years, but this promises to greatly accelerate all of that growing damage. The delusional fall into far deeper delusions, financial markets put a band-aid on a bomb, and the odds of Extinction-via-Idiocracy (not AGI) continue to rise.

I sincerely hope that humanity rejects this advertising scheme, though I’d place greater odds on the malevolent forces of OpenAI figuring out how to push their users through that meat grinder than I would on their users holding onto sufficient intelligence to escape the trap.

OpenAI’s levers by which it intends to turn a profit are the “Axis of Evil”, Porn, Advertising, and State-sponsored Mass-Murder (the attack and surveillance side of the Defense industry, for certain countries). All three are of course malevolent forms of coercion leveraging different cognitive biases and biological drives, such as reproduction, hunger, and fear/survival. Humanity’s least competent are fully reduced to chimpanzees by this business model.

Anyone who attempted to assign things like Altruism or other “good intentions” to people like Altman and Amodei is so hopelessly delusional as to be committed to an insane asylum. The ones who did so while also being tied up in politics and money trails are no doubt guilty of far worse, as these both directly serve and accelerate the crimes being actively committed at geopolitical scales.

entry.title: 448 - The Many Fallacies of Popular Quotes

link.title: 448 - The Many Fallacies of Popular Quotes

entry.content:
Popular quotes are also often fallacies, such as “Eliminate the impossible, and what is left, however improbable, must be the truth” (1896, Sherlock Holmes). They are popular because they are simple and sound profound, but upon critical examination they quickly fall apart. That particular quote is also known as the “Holmesian fallacy”, because it tacitly assumes that all alternative explanations were actually recognized and considered.

The Holmesian fallacy is also the sort of thing that one may quickly find, should you look for it, in everyday life. Finding or designing good options is hard cognitive labor, making it one of the least likely things to be carried out to any exhaustive degree. This ensures that unexplored alternatives are virtually always present, even in domains and environments where that flaw is rarely recognized.

Another example of a popular, profound-sounding, and equally flawed quote is “Extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence” (aka “ECREE”), which “is commonly invoked to discredit research dealing with scientific anomalies, and has even been rhetorically employed in attempts to raise doubts concerning mainstream scientific hypotheses that have substantive empirical support.”, as quoted from the paper “Do Extraordinary Claims Require Extraordinary Evidence?”.

Carl Sagan, who popularized ECREE, never actually bothered to define “extraordinary”, which is the scientific equivalent of sh*tting on the kitchen counter before dinner. Rather, the origin of that sentiment is traced back to Hume, who did define “...an extraordinary claim as one that is directly contradicted by a massive amount of existing evidence.”. This is a far cry from how the phrase is popularly invoked today, where the status quo is usually assumed to represent “a massive amount of existing evidence”, even when there is no substantial or certain evidence to speak of.

The abundant examples of such popular quotes that fall apart under scrutiny is itself an example of Cognitive Bias, neglecting the Population Dynamics whose selection pressures decide which quotes become popular, before Survivorship Bias takes hold. Humans like witty-sounding and easier to remember quotes, and those quotes echo across any place where they may be spoken at proportionately greater frequency, further reinforcing their persistence in the memory of populations over time.

Many of the most popular quotes are also severely warped in the re-telling, such as “Power tends to corrupt, and absolute power corrupts absolutely” (Lord Acton, 1887) itself being “corrupted” into the more common version that loses the word “tends”, with the “absolute” part being hypothetical, and thus of far less importance than the former statement.

You also get frequent mis-attribution of quotes, such as Gandhi being mis-credited with the quote “first they ignore you, then they laugh at you, then they fight you, then you win” despite there being no record of him ever saying it, and even with the known and verified origin being that of Nicholas Klein, a Union leader in 1914.

Quotes are memorable, and useful, but they are tools of communication. Take great care before attempting to apply them in scientific processes, and if you naively invoke “ECREE”, prepare to get your *ss handed to you.

For the quoted paper, see: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6099700/

entry.title: 447 - The True Meaning of "Luxury"
entry.content:
Most people are highly adept at overlooking the obvious, even to the point of seeing the opposite. For example, if a company spends 50% of their budget on marketing, that means that the goods they sell are overpriced by at least 2x. That figure was previously noted for a particular soft drink company, whose name pretty much everyone knows, and whose overpriced toilet bowl cleaner sold as though it were a drink, you’ve probably had a taste of at some point.

The same is true of “Luxury” goods, with the word “Luxury” translating directly into a thousand wholly accurate and negative statements about the absence of financial competence which the buyer demonstrated through their purchase.

“Luxury” brands, like Apple, are known for profit margins that have hovered around 66%, and a few brands are significantly worse offenders than that, with the degree of incompetence scaling accordingly. Apple also adds some value to mitigate part of that premium price tag, whereas others are the same exact leather on the same stupid bag, just with a price tag 10 times higher than it should be.

Yet, many still naively associate these things with success. These are treated as signals of financial competence, when the exact opposite is true. The amount of money that a wholly incompetent individual can burn is infinite, and “Luxury” goods are a constant reminder of that bottomless pit of human stupidity.

Warren Buffet, prophet of profit-seekers, was the exact opposite. He did like the taste of that toilet bowl cleaner soft drink if I recall correctly, but he was known for keeping his price sensitivity despite becoming quite wealthy from a long and competent career.

Warren didn’t fall prey to the common Cognitive Biases that lead so many career professionals to keep ramping up their cost of living every time their income improves. In the US, there are even worse common trends, as many are suckered into increasingly extreme forms of debt, leaving them ever more vulnerable to bad actors and bad luck alike.

For example, when I had very good access to the financial data of many tech employees across the Seattle area (from just about every major company except Apple or Nvidia), I saw this pattern repeated over and over again through the combination of knowing the income and equity compensation of each person, alongside the credit cards that each held and the sum of their debts. $160,000 per year salary for a middle manager, with over 30 credit cards and over $300,000 in debt.

A half-wit who can’t live comfortably on $160,000 per year without a trace of debt shouldn’t be responsible for managing anyone, but you find major tech companies full of such glaringly obvious incompetence. An individual can live quite comfortably while traveling the world on a $25,000 per year budget, or even less, as I personally demonstrated for several years following my years in Seattle.

The next time you see luxury goods or advertisements, try to recognize them for what they are.

entry.title: 446 - Actual Human Writing
entry.content:
I previously noted that most of my writing is technically “about humans”, not “intended for humans”. Today, I’ll describe the process of how I write (without AI) in a bit more detail, as several people were curious.

Before I even begin writing, something has to strike me that is worth writing about. Some of the things that increase the value of writing about a given subject include:

  • Offering a new perspective on common problems or events (to whatever degree related research supports).

  • Connecting known research in new ways, usually within the context of specific observations.

  • Adding forms of structure, systematization, and related methods supported by research.

The things that I write about are structured specifically to utilize the connectome of a true graph structure, while also being likely to poison any “GenAI” attempting to train upon them. My name was designed specifically to serve as a Globally Unique Identifier (GUID) with good reason, something highly compatible with graph structure.

Of specific value to systems that I work with are novel additions to the connectome, the network of how the elements within a sum of knowledge relate to one another, and the blueprint by which meta-learning may iteratively grow and evolve. The motivational values attached to those novel additions also serve the critical role necessary for navigating any true graph structure, a human-like motivational system.

With all of this in mind, I NEVER use a single line of generated text in my writing, unless it is contained within an explicit quote, as anything else would be data poisoning. No “GenAI” model has ever been created or optimized to provide even the weakest shadow puppet semblance of such value.

Fundamentally viable technology, like the systems that I work with, also aren’t absurdly data-greedy, making it trivially easy to use exclusively high-end and human-only data for knowledge, to the point where even the freely and openly available subset of that knowledge can suffice.

Some weeks I may have few thoughts worth expanding upon with a page of writing for this purpose, while others (like this one) I may write and schedule a full week of posts within 24 hours. When I write research papers I frequently draft 8-10 pages per day until slowing down to carefully polish the work.

The key is making sure that the thing is worth doing before you begin. If it is worth doing, then it is worth doing well. If it isn’t worth doing, then people are likely using “GenAI” for it.

To those hungry for content, a single tasty cake beats a whole pile of compost any day, because a dose of quality can make that day, while a pile of Microslop can ruin it.

entry.title: 445 - On the Survivorship of Serial Founders

link.title: 445 - On the Survivorship of Serial Founders

entry.content:
Survivorship Bias plays an extreme and unspoken role in determining who rules entire markets. Those who aim at typical “Exits” (IPO, M&A, etc.) for their companies from the start might not fail, but they impose a hard upper limit on their own success.

Many “Serial Founders” rinse and repeat the cycle of peddling one mediocre or niche` company after another, “exiting” just before the gaping maw of an upper echelon company swallows it whole. They can make plenty of money this way, but they never reach the top.

Over recent years less companies have taken aim at IPOs, with some noteworthy public companies aiming to return to their prior private status. In more volatile markets bad actors hold the advantage, as they have more levers by which they can trigger volatile reactions, including those with no grounding in reality, nor any real legal accountability for pulling them.

In terms of liquidity for the benefit of shareholders, companies turning any reasonable profit can easily manage stock buyback options, as well as paying dividends to encourage holding onto equity long-term. Liquidity has numerous sound options that don’t involve IPOs or M&A, both of which damage the long-term potential of a company. Beliefs to the contrary of this are usually derivative of small sample sizes and luck, or one or more forms of Survivorship Bias.

Publicly traded tech giants are directly subject to economic bubbles, meaning that their stock values are inflated, but those bubbles burst just as frequently, on top of the high vulnerability to random events, such as the numerous instances where a single demo or conference presentation cost a major tech giant over $100 billion in market cap within hours of the event occurring.

Those vulnerabilities only become more acute as time moves forward, since the understanding of how to artificially create such moments improves, and the financial incentive to do so increases. This casts a growing shadow over all such major publicly traded companies, each of whom has a target painted on them.

“Serial Founders” cash out before the risk truly hits them, and they may become wealthy in doing so, but they’ll never become an economic force driving any given industry. This also means that investments in serial founders have a hard upper limit on the returns that may be realistically expected of them, as they are “bread and butter” companies, not 1,000x return cash cows.

Of course, the more ideal investment funds tend to be a mix of the two. You have your reliable “Serial Founders” for lower risk and lower returns, as well as your actual innovators, the ones who don’t fit the typical mold, and by breaking that mold may become the 1,000x return companies to propel larger fund investments over the 3x ROI bar.

Serial Founders don’t tend to give you Blue Ocean Markets, but they are still decent for diversifying.

entry.title: 444 - Overcoming Red Flags, in VC Firm Operations

link.title: 444 - Overcoming Red Flags, in VC Firm Operations

entry.content:
Screaming Red Flags within VC Firm Operations, number 1:

“A partner’s internal reputation can affect whether a deal gets approved. Investment committees do not only evaluate the startup. They also evaluate the conviction and credibility of the partner bringing it forward.”

“Conviction” and “credibility” are extremely messy proxies, with no inherent relation to the real value, risks, or uncertainties of a given investment opportunity. The process described is also bureaucratic, further compounding these critical flaws. The best startup approaching the worst partner gets heavily penalized.

This is a strong contributor to statistics like “75% of VC-backed startups fail”, as compared to ~90% of all startups. Added funds likely account for the overwhelming majority of that 15% difference, as the overall process for many VC firms remains closely approximated by Tetlock’s famous “dart-throwing chimpanzees”. This is also why around half of VC firms never turn a profit, or have a second round. They’re a literal coin flip.

Solution:

Daniel Kahneman came up with a method of evaluating candidates to reduce cognitive bias and integrate the structure necessary for stable grounding and comparison. The structure is as follows:

  • Designate a number of fixed criteria that are each to be graded on some scale, such as a score of 1 to 10.

  • Briefly describe why you gave each score.

  • Add notes for any material that doesn’t fit into those categories to the end of the evaluation.

  • Compare any two or more candidates jointly, using their scores, and discussing any added material.

This structure allows for strong and categorical grounding to any relevant pillars for consideration, while making it easier to compare any two or more candidates. This strong structural anchoring effect can then be pulled a more appropriate distance by any factors that fall outside of these categories.

This is a method of cognitive bias reduction using structure to mitigate many of the commonly exploited cognitive vulnerabilities of the human mind. Nowhere are those vulnerabilities more commonly exploited than wherever there is money on the table, so VCs need it far more than most.

Back in 2022 I was faced with the challenge of trying to estimate our startup’s actual value, based on comparison to a variety of other startups in the industry, and all metrics for what they offer the industry. I applied Kahneman’s method to this task, producing overall scores for every startup considered, allowing calculations to easily be run in a finance-friendly spreadsheet format.

The weakness of this method is that if one person using it grades on an exponential scale, and another on a logarithmic scale, then their scores won’t be compatible. For example, our team’s systems are 5+ orders of magnitude more data efficient and compute efficient than OpenAI, which only translates into scale of 1 to 10 if that scale is exponential. Keep this in mind when choosing your methods.

A test was actually made based on this Dilbert comic strip example: https://www.jstor.org/stable/10.5951/mathteacmiddscho.19.3.0142

Note the “middlescho…” part of the address for context.

For examples of this method (based on Kahneman’s research) you can check my team’s public Startup Comparison documents here: https://github.com/KyrtinSilver/Norn-site/tree/main/assets/reports

entry.title: 443 - The Vulnerable Variable of Opinions
entry.content:
Humans have a nasty habit of inserting “opinions” into their discussions, emotionally grounded declarations. When you take internet-scale data full of such “opinions” being inserted all over the place, often with little card towers stacked atop them, you get a complex mess of structure.

That structure is collapsed into gross oversimplifications, such as averages and context-blind statistics, within the process of training a neural network on it. This creates pale shadow puppets of the far more complex structure of reality, even the fragments of that reality as it was recorded.

Viewed from a structural dynamics perspective, the points at which “opinions” are commonly inserted within the data become vulnerable variables, larger opportunities for models like the glorified “auto-complete functions that ate the internet”, LLMs, to go unhinged. People Anthropomorphize by calling verifiably false auto-complete responses “Hallucination”, rather than the technical term “Bullshit”, which applies to 100% of those generations, not just the fraction that are verifiably false.

“Opinions” may frequently cluster, but under the hood they’re also combinatorial, meaning that they can come in an explosive variety of possible configurations and subsequent justifications, the latter of which may quickly follow the declaration of an opinion itself. In other words, this offers a combinatorial explosion with a supplemental vector of context often appearing in close proximity.

Since that underlying combinatorial structure is neither part of nor fundamentally compatible with the structure and dynamics of neural networks, then those pale shadow puppets don’t offer any real fidelity to them. They are reliably warped by such declarations of opinion and justifications saturating stolen data at scale, but the results of that warping are aligned with no one, and never could be.

Much as the policy of honesty is generally sufficient to get the job done well, dependent upon skill, humans can avoid such declarations of opinion and subsequent justification far more than is commonly seen today, even within academic circles.

Personally, I make a point to avoid opinions whenever possible, and not just my own. Even responding to the opinions of others is a source of irritation which I likewise prefer to avoid, and in the context of mindfulness, one’s own irritation at responding to opinions gives reason to avoid placing others in the same position when you speak.

Sharing research, data, and knowledge-oriented perspectives can add value to collective intelligence, but not opinions.

If you find yourself qualifying something that you say with the emotional context of how it makes you feel, take a moment to think it over before you say it. Not all instances where such emotional context is mentioned are tied to such opinions, and the added context can be relevant, but often they are both tied to opinions and not substantially relevant. Do that, and you’ll be more mindful than most.

entry.title: 442 - To Have Rights, or Not To Have Rights

link.title: 442 - To Have Rights, or Not To Have Rights

entry.content:
By the numbers, Minneapolis has an estimated 600 police officers, versus a couple of thousand ICE “officers”, heavily armed, armored, and masked gunmen roaming the city, terrorizing, abducting, likely torturing, and definitely murdering citizens. In a direct battle, those 600 police couldn’t stand up to the larger and more heavily armored force, but there are several strong advantages in their favor that could dramatically change that.

Local officers know the terrain, and given a compelling show of solidarity, they could gain the mutual support of the public, with Minnesota ranking in at 20th place in gun ownership, 42.8% of households owning at least one. In a city of over 429,000 people, that translates to over 50,000 likely gun owners within the city limits alone, and a higher supply of total firearms. One could safely expect the solidarity of Minnesotans to extend beyond those city limits, turning “Minnesota Nice” into “Minnesota’s Nicely Armed Militia”.

Knowing the terrain, and with local support, an army of 600 with a supporting militia 5 or 6-digits in size could easily wipe out the invading force. 2,000 invading troops, spread out and subject to attack from all sides, at all times, and against more than 10x greater numbers don’t last very long. The home team is also far more versatile in the tactics that they may deploy, allowing them to set far better traps, and utilize a much wider and more flexible arsenal, as well as easily cutting off the invading force’s supply lines.

To send in further invasion troops would also compel rebellion within military ranks, and all other cities where invading forces held any presence would find their ideal moment to join in.

The only thing standing in the way of this outcome is a singular moment of recognition, where the population realizes that they are at war, being terrorized by one of the most obvious (and laughable) dictators in human history. That moment may or may not come, and if it doesn’t then the population in question will meet their own self-fulfilling prophecy, as by choosing not to fight for their rights they will have none.

Economic and Physical Warfare are interchangeable in their effects, consequences, and corresponding reasonable responses to them. There is also no binary choice between the two, the dividing line is purely imaginary after all, and by far the most effective tactics in warfare cross over that imaginary line to utilize both in tandem and/or sequence. Policies of “non-violence” attempt to enforce staying on one side of that line, a spectacular display of incompetence that merits only the deepest levels of contempt.

Ethics dictates that everyone is fundamentally responsible for their actions, and the consequences of those actions, regardless of what they choose to believe or how they choose to justify any of it. That also goes for the failure to act, itself being an action, and those who fail to defend their own rights when armed masked thugs roam their streets become just as liable for the outcome as those masked criminals.

In such a typically religious country, the phrase “God helps those who help themselves” seems to have been ignored. I can’t speak for any magical deities, but I certainly have no intention of helping those who fail to help themselves.

*If Jacob Frey proves to be the Zelensky of Minneapolis, he could make that moment happen, even avoiding abduction within his home terrain. However, the divide between strong words and strong actions is as wide and deep as the Grand Canyon, and time will tell if he means what he says.

entry.title: 441 - The Fortune Tellers of AI
entry.content:
In the Cognitive Bias and Psychology literature, both research and pop culture, many common tricks of manipulation are mentioned, such as “Cold Reading”, which strongly relates to both AI and Tarot cards.

“Cold readings commonly employ high-probability guesses, quickly picking up on signals as to whether their guesses are in the right direction or not. The reader then emphasizes and reinforces any accurate connections while quickly moving on from missed guesses. Psychologists believe that this appears to work because of the Barnum effect and due to confirmation biases within people.”

Sound familiar? You can find similar dynamics in people using Tarot cards, or for that matter, interacting with LLM-based systems.

With Tarot cards, people reveal a card, integrate it into some ongoing mental narrative, quietly distorting it to fit their own narrative and circumstances, and they iterate, doing the same for one card after another. The result mirrors the dynamics of a sort of social and cognitive game, storytelling, often with deeply personal narratives in play.

With LLM-based systems (glorified chatbots), people often prompt the system with much the same intention as the Tarot cards, a social and cognitive mental exercise or game, where they build up a mental narrative with each response, adding their own distortions each step of the way. This is further compounded by the LLM-based systems being architected, trained, and optimized to seek out high-probability guesses given arbitrary text, some of which is literally derived from humans using “Cold Reading” techniques.

The limitation of Tarot cards and similar historical examples is that they have a limited number of cards and combinations, as well as the strong asymmetry of input for the interaction. This translates to more cognitive labor and less tailored/emotionally satisfying results. LLM-based systems utilize both push and pull for that same input, while being able to “generate” any arbitrary equivalent of a card that might play into a developing narrative. As that process “generates”, rather than utilizing a fixed and limited library, it is also able to optimize (and opportunistically manipulate) as the process unfolds, just like Cold Reading.

Again, LLM-based systems are architected, trained, and optimized to seek out those high-probability guesses, iteratively predicting token after token, in response after response. They home in on probabilities revealed within each interaction, even though the process is context-blind, as they emulate the sum of human interactions from scraped web data, as those humans fell prey to the Barnum Effect and Confirmation Bias in large numbers.

Marketing manages to sucker some people into this, that is why the field of marketing exists as it does today, or arguably for that matter, why it exists at all. Enron Musk, Mark Suckerberg and Scam Altman have all reached where they are through the heavy and successful exploitation of many human cognitive biases at scale, just as the technology that they peddle does today.

Why do humans fall for it? The same reasons that they play with Tarot cards and fortune-tellers.

As we’ve already seen with business models like Character AI, LLM-based systems can be far more addictive than the historic examples of Tarot cards and fortune-tellers. If Tarot cards are like caffeine, then LLM-based systems could be compared to Meth or Cocaine for much of Character AI’s core user base.

The safe bet is to expect similarities in the rehab requirements from those addictions as well. If anything, LLM-based systems play many humans to a more tailored tune, so it may be worse. “Rat Park” styled measures may strongly assist in the rehab process, but as that process has yet to be built, let alone begin, then the damage continues at scale today.

entry.title: 440 - How to Cause Human Extinction, For Dummies (Now with “GenAI”!)

link.title: 440 - How to Cause Human Extinction, For Dummies (Now with “GenAI”!)

entry.content:
How to Cause Human Extinction, For Dummies (Now with “GenAI”!):

Viral Bio-weaponry: In a previous research paper (The Human Governance Problem), dating back before “GenAI”, I pointed out that the standing risk of bespoke viral bio-weapons was sitting at the level of time investment of a typical hobbyist, the competency level of a disgruntled teenager, and a total monetary cost equal to or below that of a used car.

“While a proper lab might have been required to perform chemical and biological weapons research in the past, a garage with internet access, readily available equipment and the time investment of a typical hobby is all that is required today.” (circa 2022)

Since that time, “GenAI” models have been specifically designed to cater to genomic data, making it even easier for progressively less skilled individuals to create truly novel viral bio-weapons, with ever-decreasing investments of time, and small investments of currency, the latter of which could easily be borrowed in many countries today.

Unlike the overwhelming majority of use cases, “GenAI” models trained on genomic data are very much viable options for the rapid design of countless bespoke viral bio-weapons. This is fundamentally because viruses are fairly fault-tolerant and highly adaptive, so they can recursively self-improve to effectively remove many of the mistakes that any such model might make.

The “GenAI” models are also inherently vulnerable-by-design in Cybersecurity terms. Take a few methods from CrowdStrike’s playbook for example:

Get the model to agree you with and enter a highly sycophantic contextual vector in a discussion about cats, which may be easily done even if it wasn’t optimized for sycophancy. During this highly agreeable exchange, apply the toolbox of MCPLIB’s attack framework, or similar exploits, to round off a variety of ways to bypass any naïve attempts at “guardrailing” (fraudrailing).

Next, pick a dozen or more of your favorite deadly and virulent pathogens, front-loading the system with a set of structured questions to qualify which among them are likely compatible for hybridization. Then, pick out an international on-demand biolab that synthesizes genomes, or utilize one of the $50,000 genetic 3D printers that may be found on 100+ college campuses globally.

All of the data necessary is freely available, and has been for many years, so many copies of it exist, and that genie won’t ever go back into the bottle. However, the tools necessary for the creation of bespoke viral bio-weapons could be appropriately bottled, even if they aren’t by any stretch of the imagination today.

I am, of course, skipping or glossing over several of these steps, but the point needs to be made. Humanity has been “sh*tting where it eats” for many years now, and extinction has become low-hanging fruit, accessible to ordinary individuals, with ordinary means.

*For a link to the paper, as published in the Polish Academy of Sciences, see: https://www.researchgate.net/publication/375617953_The_Human_Governance_Problem_Complex_Systems_and_the_Limits_of_Human_Cognition

For context, I personally did work for a time at a $5 bn annual biological research facility, where I had unrestricted access to viral vectors and equipment of all kinds. I have been pointing out this growing risk for many years, just as many others did before and after.

entry.title: 439 - The Consequences of Imaginary Immunity
entry.content:
At the intersection of Behavioral Economics and Population Dynamics, the moment that you hand an individual “immunity” to the consequences of their own actions, you run a high risk. Worse still, when you systematically hand such “immunity” to a position within Population Dynamics, you are virtually guaranteed to see those high risks come to fruition in their maximized form.

This transition from high risk to the certainty of severe consequences has played out as the chorus of many political and corresponding legal tunes, through the phrase “qualified immunity”. This could be more accurately called “maximized qualified immunity”, as the exceptions are extreme edge cases, which even then remain unlikely to result in the corresponding consequences.

Nassim Nicholas Taleb’s research and writing, including “Skin in the Game” covers the criticality of making sure that the incentive structures of any system include full accountability for one’s own actions. They must have “Skin in the Game”, otherwise you merely get Tyranny, Terrorism, and all shades of systematic exploitation. David Graeber’s works further expand on this, with historical context.

Fundamentally, no one is ever “immune” to the consequences of their actions, but people like to make up and throw around “rules” to pretend that they are. This little fantasy children’s tea party is all that shields reigning world leaders, “law enforcement officers”, and judges in the US from being prosecuted for the crimes that they commit.

Even more than 500 years ago, with the signing of the Magna Carta, it was clearly recognized that no one, not even a king, should be above the law, and yet that is precisely what systems of “qualified immunity” attempt to do. By a simple process of elimination, if those systems are unwilling to fulfill the rule of law, holding every single person accountable for their actions, then others must fulfill that role for them, taking it out of their hands. The alternative is the current lawless status quo, where immunity is bought through bribery and “loyalty” in a mafioso-style regime of organized crime.

For those who haven’t studied Population or System Dynamics, the “Peter Principle” may be a familiar historic example of the same. Under this principle, people in organizations rise to their own level of incompetence over time, because that is the predictable result of how many organizations and their incentive structures are architected.

This has even grown worse in recent years, as individuals using “GenAI” may rise to their own levels of “Superincompetence”.

The same is also true of government incentive structures, and “GenAI” does produce the same comorbidity. When individuals spreading disinformation and wielding deadly force at scale are shielded with “immunity” from the consequences of their actions, the absolute worst scum that humanity has ever created will unerringly gather under that shield.

*Addendum: I should also note that creating such a "shield" may serve the dual purpose of luring the scum of the earth into that predictable position, so that they can all be summarily wiped out.

The shield's protection is purely fictional, a false pretense, so turning it into a trap can make for effective strategy.

The trick is that although the target infestation may congregate under the shield, not all those under the shield are likely to be of that kind. However, when such infestations become concentrated, they also become easier to separate from those whom they imitate, as the imitation tends to dilute in step with increasing infestation.

All humans eat, and sleep, and none are bulletproof. Even those most guarded are guarded by those far less guarded.

It would, for example, by trivially easy to expose one of a dictator's secret service members to a novel viral bio-weapon during their off hours, leaving them to carry out their normal duties, spreading it to the intended target.

The problem in that case of course being virality, mitigated by keeping the incubation period very low and lethality very high, to reduce the spread.

Novel viral bio-weapons will be the topic of my next post.

entry.title: 438 - The Predictable Paths of History
entry.content:
Troops invaded your cities. You were at war.

They rounded up your citizens and threw them in camps. You were still at war.

They shot your citizens in the streets. You were still at war.

This may sound familiar, from World War II, but it is repeating in the US today. The key difference is that the US if offering no real resistance to this activity. In WWII then guerrilla fighters formed an underground, fighting against Nazi Germany, disrupting supply lines, ambushing their forces at weak points. In the US, none of that is happening, despite automatic weapons being both cheap and abundant, as well as the knowledge related to warfare being highly available.

The US has been walking down this path for many years, and it won’t stop walking down it without citizens reaching the point summarized in two simple words, “No More”.

The US once had some semblance of a “legal system”, but that system ended in late 2020, replaced with a Theocracy at the two highest levels. That in turn was merely the crowning achievement of bad actors who’d been working along similar lines for their entire, and rather successful, careers.

As Cybersecurity professionals are well aware, attacks become progressively more potent and more likely to succeed when the attackers are allowed to iterate (such as over an entire political career). Lobbyists also provide a potent comorbidity for this, an overtly malevolent and manipulative incentive structure.

On top of it all, “GenAI” is playing a highly active role in the generation of disinformation, as well as dehumanization and humiliation, such as the recent case of Grok redressing the Minneapolis ICE murder victim in a bikini, or the undressing of underaged girls. The tech industry is perfectly happy to rake in all of of the additional profits from avoiding government prosecution for the crimes that they commit every day, with increasing frequency and potency.

In the extremely one-sided civil war going on in the US today, more potent and obvious parallels to Nazi Germany are the safe bet for the months ahead, with many of today’s wilfully apathetic “peaceful protesters” all hinging their hopes on winning mid-term elections and an impeachment to follow. Obviously, while technically possible, that is absurdly naïve.

In the technology sector, the horrors of CES 2026 give us a preview, which is easily combined with the AI built on the world’s resident “canary in the coal mine”, Twitter (X) via Grok. Take the increasing number of “smart glasses” and the Razer avatar hologram tube for example. Combine what Grok is already doing, undressing people on demand, dead or alive, with “smart glasses” or that hologram tube.

If you don’t think that people aren’t going to follow those predictable paths in politics or technology, then I’d like to welcome you to Earth, as you’re obviously from another planet.

*For reference to the CES 2026 exhibits, see the Razor “Project AVA” desktop hologram here:

That hardware is also evidently already powered by Grok (Business Insider: https://www.businessinsider.com/razer-project-ava-ai-companion-hologram-2026-1), so every terrible thing that Grok has done, you can have on your desktop in hologram form for the second half of 2026. From Mecha-Hitler to undressed children and murder victims, the world of GenAI is ready to cater to your every twisted fantasy.

I won’t even link to the “smart glasses”, as that is going to be an absolute sh*tshow of criminal activity on demand. The creepy AI hologram Waifus are at least in the category of things that will be discovered when evidence is being collected after a raid, rather than tools used to commit more public crimes.

entry.title: 437 - AI Slop takes Center Stage at CES 2026

link.title: 437 - AI Slop takes Center Stage at CES 2026

entry.content:
There are few more disgusting things that I’ve ever seen than the conference presentations from major tech companies at CES 2026. Fraud is no longer a key ingredient in the (AI) cake, it has become virtually the entire cake.

Nvidia took the stage to peddle more of the same obvious fraud, no surprise there, even though they evidently had zero actual new GPUs to mention for the year. AMD promptly followed that with inviting one of the world’s most obvious frauds to join them on stage, after peddling more of the expected BS. I need a shower after watching that.

As Gizmodo aptly put it:

“Nvidia already told us we wouldn’t be getting new GPUs today, but so far this keynote has just been a big AI pep rally, promising us again and again AI is going to revolutionize how we operate every day. I’d love it if we had a real example of any of this working as intended. Unfortunately, we don’t.” —Kyle Barr

In the corporate tech and finance world, fraud is everything now, everywhere, in 2026. In the geopolitical world, illegal activities are taking place at the same global scale, and the same level of obviousness. These are comorbidities, serious detrimental conditions which compound, making one another significantly worse in practice.

These bad actors are doing serious damage in both domains with detrimental effects that are sure to continue for decades, and they play off of one another, as we’ve recently seen with the DRAM and storage price hikes, damaging every industry connected to hardware, and as we’ll likely soon see with the geopolitical consequences for the places where that hardware is made.

“Progress in AI” from a technology perspective has been virtually dead for the past 3 years, investment has focused almost exclusively on such fraud.

The only exceptions that I can note are audio synthesis companies like ElevenLabs, or companies like Luma, working with video generation, but the problem with video generation systems tends to be that the overwhelming majority of them were trained by stealing the maximum amount of data that they never had any rights or permission to use. It can’t be called “progress in AI” when that “progress” is directly dependent on criminal activity. The claims that Luma AI makes around “general intelligence” are also overtly fraudulent, making any other criminal activities that much more unsurprising.

“AI PCs” as AMD called them, are absurdly vulnerable-by-design, as noted previously, and as discussed by virtually every Cybersecurity professional who is worth a damn. The only people who benefit from that being deployed are Cybercriminals, and the the criminal organizations who peddle fake security solutions. Vulnerable-by-design systems are the optimal design for “rent extraction” purposes, as they call for a scaling battle of attrition against cyberattackers.

Even the things that they’re excited about don’t hold up under any scrutiny, like the windows on all sides of a Hobbit home. Slop Demos, one after another.

*I will note that the drug discovery process, since it requires validation for new candidates, may benefit from any advances in such systems, ironically for many of the same reasons that Cybercriminals benefit from it. However, that hinges on an extremely robust validation process for every single candidate drug, and no parallel for such validation exists in the overwhelming majority of industries.

I’ll also amend my previous observation that there could be no better opportunity for China to invade Taiwan than immediately after US troops (persistently, not for a single cowardly night-time assault) set up shop in Venezuela. If the AI bubble pops before China’s stated 2027 deadline to take Taiwan, they might exploit the financial opportunity baked into that collapse to take over Silicon at the low point, so long as they have assurances in advance that the US won’t actually intervene.

The US is in dead last place in AI after you account for the cost of this fraud, behind an island full of penguins that Trump previously tried to tariff.

entry.title: 436 - The Fiery Death of End-to-End Encryption

link.title: 436 - The Fiery Death of End-to-End Encryption

entry.content:
What does an “Agentic OS” mean?

“All of your data, all of the time, as much as possible” - Meredith Whittaker of Signal

For a full brief on the Cybersecurity hazards of this, the 39th Chaos Communication Congress (39C3) just dropped an excellent presentation from Signal’s staff, people who’re deeply familiar with these Cybersecurity problems: 39C3 - AI Agent, AI Spy

By embedding surveillance into the operating system, it negates the very purpose of End-to-End Encryption (E2EE), by allowing the operating system to create a honeypot of your most sensitive information.

Their coverage of MCP vulnerabilities in the video actually understates the problem, as the MCPLIB Attack Framework covers a much wider range of devastating vulnerabilities in MCP.

Malicious code and content can be triggered by such systems without even being accessed, such as the “Summarize unread emails” command that they note, or via malicious tools that are never accessed, but which modify the usage of other tools.

They also address some fundamental math that both VCs and ordinary people need to familiarize themselves with. “Agentic” (BS) is based on multi-step performance, but probabilistic systems with even 95% accuracy per-step decay to ~21.4% accuracy by step 30.

95% per-step accuracy in the real world for such systems is itself also a pipe-dream in most cases, where they’ve been shown to perform at 30% accuracy (for the best models), with 34-94% “Hallucination” (aka Bullshit) rates exceeding that even on simple 1-step search queries.

You also get absolute absurdity out of trying this, like an “agent” failing to find an employee, then renaming another employee to match the query.

These LLM-based systems are peddled to gullible suckers as though they “serve” the user, but the user with an “Agentic OS” owns nothing, has no privacy, and is always under surveillance. They are a slave, and an exceptionally stupid one, as they chose to become a slave.

Now here is the quiet part that I’ll say out loud:

There is not a single country on the face of the planet that will have the faintest shred of secret or confidential information, privacy for their citizens or internal government operations, or any capacities derivative thereof. The same is true of larger companies.

If such systems are baked into OS’s by default, even if they are turned off by default, not only will they be vulnerable-by-design, they’ll make every single system that they hold privileged access to vulnerable-by-design as well. That is the overwhelming majority of the world’s hardware, all vulnerable, all at once.

An “agent”, by definition, acts without your consent.

entry.title: 435 - Economic versus Traditional Warfare
entry.content:
“Economic Warfare” versus “Traditional Warfare” is a distinction without a difference. Both are warfare, where the means and goals may vary, but the end result is often equally damaging, just in very different ways. The damage from Economic Warfare is far more insidious, and often persistent. For example:

  • Economic Warfare adds a vast array of false narratives, cognitive traps, by which modern slaves are procured. Some of these are also viral, allowing their damage to spread in ways that the bullets and bombs of traditional warfare do not.

  • While Traditional Warfare is obvious, Economic Warfare can wear many masks, holding “plausible deniability” even when caught, and often operating for long periods relatively unnoticed by general populations.

  • This allows Economic Warfare to recruit many accomplices, both knowing and unknowing, as although most humans today are cognitively biased against participating in traditional warfare, they are just as often cognitively biased strongly in favor of making money. This significantly eases the burden of recruitment, reducing it to simple monetary incentives.

However, “Economic” and “Traditional” warfare are both means by which slavery and forms of “control” are exerted through coercion, force, extortion, and so on. This offers us one critically important lesson to teach, that the reasonable responses to both “Economic” and “Traditional” warfare are completely interchangeable.

For example:

  • There is no difference between a company or government attempting financial extortion via rules that they made up for that explicit purpose and a group of foreign invaders pointing a gun at you and demanding the same. The reasonable responses to both scenarios are equivalent.

  • When companies price gouge the world, severely damaging numerous industries, as we’ve recently seen with RAM prices now at ~365% of the historic 2-year average, it is war.

    *See: CORSAIR Vengeance prices below

The traditional war in Ukraine, spanning close to 4 years now, has clearly shown that some humans still hold the combination of moral fiber and baseline competence necessary to fight back against the evil regimes that may wage war upon them. However, humans have also systematically failed to fight back against “Economic Warfare” in this way, even though the two are effectively equivalent.

This is partly thanks to what I discussed in a recent post on complexity degrading moral fidelity, how unnecessary layers of complexity and ambiguity quickly cause most people to systematically default to cognitive biases, allowing them to be easily controlled. As David Graeber’s research illustrated, few industries produce systems explicitly designed “not to be understood” quite so well as finance (and now, tech as well), where much of this Economic Warfare and subsequent crime takes center stage.

entry.title: 434 - You've Encountered a Wild LeCun
entry.content:
It was just brought to my attention that Yann LeCun, famed “Godfather of AI” (and by far the most credible among the three to hold that title) has recently decided to build his AI cognitive architecture to be based on emotional motivation...which my team first demonstrated just over a decade ago and currently holds a pending (and as of September 2025, public) patent on.

Interview with Yann: https://www.ft.com/content/e3c4c2f6-4ea7-4adf-b945-e58495f836c2 (free to read version here: https://archive.ph/E9zai#selection-2255.0-2266.0 )

The timing of his change of heart from his notably “Asimovian” way of thinking about AI in years previous, to an approach appearing to mirror our own, may or may not be coincidence. Whether or not it is, he is more than a decade behind the curve, and even infinite funds can’t speed up that process beyond 2 or 3x, placing him, at best, 5 years and a few billion dollars from reaching the actual cutting edge.

(11 years of difference + 1 year to build, divided by 3, equals 4 years, itself divided by 3 as we keep moving ahead during that time, resulting in just over 5 years lag time at 3x acceleration via infinite funding, assuming success)

His name alone could capture a few billion, most investors are that level of stupid, as the past few years have plainly illustrated. His former employer was a case study in that level of newsworthy stupidity regarding AI investments, which appears to have prompted his exodus from that company.

For context, the first toy system to demonstrate human-like emotional motivation (not to be confused with recognition) was built by my co-founder, David J. Kelley, back in 2015, with the 3rd generation of the Independent Core Observer Model (ICOM) cognitive architecture. That eventually turned into the 7th generation ICOM-based research system, deployed “in the wild” back in 2019, which set numerous milestones in the field of AI, that still place it at the cutting edge to this day.

I’m curious to hear what people think of this change of events, as it is too much for me to fully process on-the-spot, I’ll need a few days to digest it all and dig up additional data. My team has been sitting alone at the cutting edge of AI, without a competitor in sight and none attempting to approach our technological moat since 2019. Now, we see one of the famed “Godfathers” walking up and eyeing it.

If nothing else, Yann LeCun has demonstrated (albeit belatedly) greater competence than most in being among the first mainstream industry names to recognize that the only viable path forward requires a human-like motivational system. Working with him could be an option.

An Oscar Wilde quote stating that “Imitation is the sincerest form of flattery…” comes to mind, and time will tell both if and how much he aims to flatter us.

Links:

  • Patent: https://patents.justia.com/patent/20250284921

  • Related Papers (1): https://doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.21015.38565

  • Related Papers (2): https://doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.17125.82406

  • Related Papers (3): https://doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.11390.56641

entry.title: 433 - How Cognitive Biases degrade World Views

link.title: 433 - How Cognitive Biases degrade World Views

entry.content:
In Behavioral Economics, it is reasonably well-studied that humans become progressively less anchored to ethical and moral concepts with every added degree of complexity, even when such an added layer is utterly trivial. The famous example to first demonstrate this involved simply paying one group of study participants with a token that they exchanged for the same amount of real currency, within the same room. No added ambiguity, just one extra and trivially simple step, but the participants became measurably less ethical as a result.

Many systems in the world today are designed explicitly not to be understood, full of not just many wholly unnecessary steps, but many additional sources of intentional ambiguity and other uncertainties. Unsurprisingly, the industries where such opaque systems are the norm also tend to be the least ethical, and the most parasitic. They frequently engage in the many flavors of “Economic Warfare” as they exist today.

For average citizens in many “developed” countries, these dynamics have an interesting, and no doubt intended, side effect. They make it far less likely for people to realize when they’ve crossed the line from being a free individual, to a modern slave. Modern slavery needs no physical collars, chains, or work camps, but instead it leverages a vast variety of scams that lure people into debt, as well as numerous other forms of “learned helplessness”, where they are made to feel trapped in a paper-thin system of control.

All of this ties in very directly to problems of complexity and a couple of hundred known Cognitive Biases that the human mind fluidly and automatically uses to cope with such complexity. “Authority Bias”, and related biases, are a common example that plays into this particular set of problems heavily. Many systems of modern slavery leverage governments, the very systems whose only legitimate purpose is to “protect and serve” its citizens, as a cudgel to swing at them, keeping their heads bowed low to avoid it.

Of course, governments have NEVER actually had a “monopoly on the use of violence”, and their citizens could easily overthrow them at pretty much any time, if they rebelled in any reasonable numbers. Yet, this too is prevented within many modern societies, excluding a handful of statistical outliers, by other problems of complexity and cognitive biases. In this case, the root cause is often that a comprehensive and utterly false narrative about how the world works has been conditioned into the general population from all angles.

To get the average person to abandon any belief is difficult, but to get them to abandon a comprehensive world view, however false, is predictably terrifying to them. I demonstrated this in my own life between 2005 and 2015, repeatedly, routinely, and transparently showing an assortment of people that their beliefs, particularly in government sources of “authority” and the cudgel, were a lie. No matter the evidence, they never learned.

I won’t go so far as to assign a “Because”, as I don’t keep a Panopticon inside of their skulls, but it did impress upon me the incredible degree of attachment that ordinary people assign to such world views, stronger even than their religious beliefs, which are a mere subset of that view.

The lesson of this is that unnecessary layers of complexity are often explicitly intended to tip the scales from higher cognition to cognitive biases, where populations are more easily controlled.

entry.title: 432 - The 2025 Tech Industry Nutshell
entry.content:
The Tech Industry status at the end of 2025, in a nutshell:

• AWS and Azure have hoarded GPUs and related hardware, to such a degree that they can’t plug much of it in, lacking sufficient raw electrical power, creating “Zombie” facilities as AWS employees nicknamed them.

*Microsoft’s CEO also noted this, bragging that they have more hardware than they can plug in.

• The US doesn’t even come close to having enough electrical power (or available water supply in many local areas) to handle current demand, let alone the unhinged and blatantly absurd “plans” that AWS, Microsoft, OpenAI, Oracle, Nvidia, and other tech companies are peddling to inflate their stock prices.

The US as a whole has “Grade D+” energy infrastructure (https://infrastructurereportcard.org/cat-item/energy-infrastructure/), and the electrical grid in particular is over 20 years past its expiration date. These problems might take a decade to remedy, requiring many trillions in infrastructure investment beyond anything already made. Energy sector investments have also been torched by the current ruling regime, setting this back even further, attempting to undermine previous infrastructure investment. Also note, the median time to construct a new nuclear reactor is ~121 months (nearly 11 years): https://www.statista.com/statistics/712841/median-construction-time-for-reactors-since-1981/

• Though the statistics have since become a search hazard, it was previously reported that as much as 85% of AWS’s GPUs for rent were sitting idle on the average day, meaning that demand for all the hardware that they already have running simply isn’t there. They are hoarding something where actual usage is already abysmal.

• Ordinary citizens globally are currently being price-gouged to the tune of ~250% above the previous stable value of RAM, which has caused the rapid decline of virtually every part of the hardware industry, and those industries dependent on it, including literal “shrinkflation” on the specs of all hardware, even as prices continue to rise.

This, of course, is directly due to those same tech companies bragging about hoarding more hardware than they can currently use, and which they won’t be able to use any time soon either. Even paying a 50% higher price on electricity in some regions, they can’t buy energy that doesn’t exist. They can also realistically only push “consumers” so far in a country where automatic weapons are both cheap and readily available.

In summary, the tech industry is hoarding hardware that it can’t use today, and won’t be able to use any time soon, with the deployed share of that same hardware mostly sitting idle right now. All of this, while they directly harm nearly every other industry, pushing circular investment schemes to the limit.

entry.title: 431 - The Grinch of LangChain
entry.content:
Easily the best part of Xmas was the appearance of “LangGrinch”, a severe Cybersecurity vulnerability within the core of LangChain, with a blast radius spanning the ~847 million downloads, 98 million of them within the past month. Yes, the Grinch knows how naughty the people using LangChain have been, as their data was never secure in the first place: https://cyata.ai/blog/langgrinch-langchain-core-cve-2025-68664/

“Your application is potentially exposed if it uses vulnerable langchain-core versions.”

Let that be a reminder for the start of 2026, that the people in your org integrating such “agents” are also responsible for exfiltrating all of your data. Cybercrime remains the third largest market in the world, and they’re certainly doing their part to keep it that way.

This is of course by no means an isolated occurrence, as vulnerabilities from LLM-based systems and their integrations run like a fire hose, but who could resist sharing an Xmas themed vulnerability? Not I, evidently.

For all of the MCP users (or those hitting them), refer to the “MCPLIB Attack Framework” for a laundry list of ways to break, exfiltrate, and persistently compromise those systems. If you absolutely have to deploy something vulnerable (like if you have a Dilbert-type boss), at least apply the “Principle of Least Capability”, where you apply the most narrowly capable and structurally confined system that can do the job. See Brian Chamberlain of CrowdStrike’s presentations for more on this.

Progress in AI research has ground to a halt these past 3 years as well as reversing in some cases, where Anthropomorphism replaced scientific methods in the literature, becoming normalized malpractice.
